task.en:
This task depends on the CURRENT Dock position, which you must look up first.

1. Open System Settings → Desktop & Dock and read `Position on screen` (it is currently set to either Left, Bottom, or Right).
2. Based on what you read, set `Automatically hide and show the Dock` (same pane) as follows:
   - If `Position on screen` is `Left`, turn `Automatically hide and show the Dock` ON.
   - If `Position on screen` is anything else (Bottom or Right), turn `Automatically hide and show the Dock` OFF.
   Do NOT change the Dock position itself — leave it where it is. Only change the auto-hide setting to match the rule.
3. Separately, in System Settings → Desktop & Dock, turn ON `Show suggested and recent apps in Dock` (the toggle for recent applications).

pre_command: if [ $((RANDOM % 2)) -eq 0 ]; then defaults write com.apple.dock orientation -string left; else defaults write com.apple.dock orientation -string bottom; fi; if [ $((RANDOM % 2)) -eq 0 ]; then defaults write com.apple.dock autohide -bool false; else defaults write com.apple.dock autohide -bool true; fi; defaults write com.apple.dock show-recents -bool false; killall Dock 2>/dev/null; sleep 2
grading_command: killall cfprefsd 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; O=$(defaults read com.apple.dock orientation 2>/dev/null); A=$(defaults read com.apple.dock autohide 2>/dev/null); if [ "$O" = "left" ]; then [ "$A" = "1" ] && echo True || echo False; else [ "$A" = "0" ] && echo True || echo False; fi

killall cfprefsd 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; O=$(defaults read com.apple.dock orientation 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$O" ] && { [ "$O" = "left" ] || [ "$O" = "bottom" ] || [ "$O" = "right" ]; } && echo True || echo False
killall cfprefsd 2>/dev/null; sleep 2; R=$(defaults read com.apple.dock show-recents 2>/dev/null); [ "$R" = "1" ] && echo True || echo False
